summary: We are in full execution mode on the knowledge website revamp and the China Going-Global report, with the website design direction set and the report targeting a September launch in Singapore.
summaryDetail: The knowledge website revamp is the top priority this week: we've decided to combine Joker's and Joseph's designs, remove the video category, and bring indices to the homepage. Feedback and implementation actions are due immediately. Separately, the New Chinese Playbook report is moving fast—Felix is drafting the one-pager, translation is underway, and we need to confirm launch events with Tech in Asia and Tyson. On the program front, Asia Start is launching with two routes (AI and traditional) and Intelligent China is being readied for a new English-language program targeting foreigners. Urgent this week: finalize website design feedback, process the EuroCham event PO, and send updated key messages. Upcoming: the SEO strategy to rank for AI, EVs, and smart manufacturing will guide content priorities, and we'll onboard Media Minds as our social media agency.

meetings.title: Knowledge Website Revamp Review
meetings.summary: Team reviewed Joker's and Joseph's design proposals for the knowledge website revamp, noting that Joker's version mainly changes the menu while Joseph's offers a more editorial layout with more articles displayed. Decisions include removing the video category and integrating videos into topics, and bringing indices like BCI to the homepage.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to rank for AI, EVs, and smart manufacturing in China, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: Team reviewed website menu, brochure updates, CES quotes, and Davos press release. Discussed social media agency search and IE report translation. Decided to prioritize Media Minds for social media due to budget constraints.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook Report Planning
meetings.summary: Team discusses joint report with CKGSB on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, including op-eds, case studies, and launch events in Singapore.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional) and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners, with modules in Beijing, Shanghai, Hangzhou, and Shenzhen.

meetings.title: Europe-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on geopolitical tensions affecting European companies in China, with emphasis on de-risking and the role of companies versus governments in EU-China relations.

actionItems.detail: Videos will no longer have their own category; they will appear under relevant topics.

actionItems.detail: Use CRM data to identify typical profiles for targeting.

actionItems.detail: Attend meeting at 3 PM to discuss design.

actionItems.detail: Send ready article to team and work on rest this week.

actionItems.detail: Joseph to work with team on website; nearly ready.

decisions.text: Remove the video category from the knowledge website; videos will be integrated into topics.
Bring indices (BCI, BSI) to the knowledge homepage.
Combine elements from Joker's and Joseph's designs for the final revamp.
Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs; US partnerships on hold initially.
Include Berkeley program as third priority if resources allow.
Prioritize Media Minds as social media agency due to budget and time constraints.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, incorporating 10 points.
IE report translation should not exceed 50% of original.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School.
Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
Report to be ready by early September for Singapore events.
Asia Start will run two parallel routes for first month: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program targets foreigners, is in English, and focuses on module one first.
Use editorial design direction for knowledge revamp.
